title: In Paris, Reza Pahlavi, son of Iran's last shah, builds his network
subhed: Reza Pahlavi has been based in the French capital since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran started, making moves in anticipation of the Islamic Republic's fall.

In Sicily, anger erupts after US Navy helicopters land in a protected area
subhed: Local officials condemned the use of military aircraft on a UNESCO-listed site, highlighting the ambiguity of Giorgia Meloni's stance on the war waged by the US and Israel.

title: French singer Patrick Bruel faces rape inquiry, says prosecutor
subhed: The 66-year-old is the latest in a string of French movie and music celebrities to face sexual assault claims. Bruel's lawyer Christophe Ingrain told Agence France-Presse that the actor and singer 'maintains he never rejected a refusal (to have sex), never forced anyone into a sexual act or relationship.'

title: At the Arab World Institute, an exhibition on Byblos, Lebanon's millennia-old city, loses key artwork due to war
subhed: Precious objects attesting to the cultural wealth of the ancient port city have, for security reasons, not made the journey to Paris, while others crossed borders under military protection.

title: Mark Carney's delicate balancing act at Canada's helm
subhed: The pragmatic former banker captivated the world with his speech in Davos on the autonomy of middle powers in the face of hegemonic giants. But the political footing of this tightrope walker remains difficult to define.

subhed: The 2026 municipal elections have already concluded in the vast majority of French towns, with a mayor being directly elected in the first round. In larger cities, however, the right and the Socialists are in the lead for the second round.

title: Violence in restaurant kitchens exposes deep wounds in fine dining
subhed: The recent allegations against renowned Danish chef René Redzepi highlight the urgent need for a profound overhaul of management practices in the restaurant industry. Initiatives are spreading in France to break the silence surrounding abuse in the sector.
